basements__swimming_pools___general_ques.details_of_future_basement_work_planned_: Design and construction management of a new 1,200 sq ft reinforced concrete basement level beneath an existing detached residence at 1845 Oakridge Drive, Reading, including excavation, underpinning, waterproofing and internal fit-out.

Scope covers structural design coordination with the engineer, temporary works design review, selection and oversight of a Type C drained cavity waterproofing system, and liaison with building control and the warranty provider; works are scheduled to start in 2025-03 with an anticipated 32-week programme.
basements__swimming_pools___general_ques.what_service_did_you_provide_line_1: Full project management and principal designer services under CDM Regulations for the construction of an indoor heated concrete swimming pool (10m x 4m) as part of a wider high-end residential refurbishment in Surrey, including coordination of architectural, structural and M&E design teams.
basements__swimming_pools___general_ques.what_service_did_you_provide_line_2: Responsibilities included developing the client brief, preparing detailed programmes and risk registers, overseeing tendering and contractor selection, chairing monthly progress and health & safety meetings, monitoring quality of reinforced concrete shell, waterproofing and finishes, and coordinating commissioning and handover documentation for the pool and associated plant room.

largest_contracts___contract_1_details.contract_1_who_were_you_contracted_to: Hawthorne Residential Developments Ltd, 52 Market Street, Guildford, Surrey GU1 3JT
largest_contracts___contract_1_details.contract_1_who_was_the_end_client_for_th: Private client family office of Jonathan and Emily Cartwright, c/o Cartwright Family Trust, 7 New Cavendish Street, London W1G 8UU
largest_contracts___contract_1_details.contract_1_what_was_being_built_changed_: Design and construction of a new two-storey basement and luxury leisure suite beneath an existing detached dwelling known as "Oakwood House" in Cobham, Surrey. The works comprised full excavation and temporary works to form a double-height basement under the footprint of the house and part of the rear garden, including an indoor swimming pool (12m x 5m), spa pool, sauna, steam room, gym, cinema room, plant rooms, and ancillary storage. The project involved significant structural alterations to the existing house, installation of a comprehensive waterproofing system (combined Type A and Type C), new mechanical ventilation and air-conditioning, high-end interior finishes, bespoke joinery and lighting, external landscaping reinstatement, and integration of the new basement level with the existing ground and first floors via a new feature staircase and passenger lift.
largest_contracts___contract_1_details.contract_1_what_goods_or_services_did_yo:
My company, Meridian Basement & Leisure Projects Ltd, acted as project manager and contract administrator for the client-side development company, Hawthorne Residential Developments Ltd. Our services covered the full pre-construction and construction phases, including: 

- Initial feasibility review of forming a two-storey basement under the existing structure, with coordination of ground investigation works, review of soil reports and existing foundations, and early-stage risk assessment for ground movement and impact on neighbouring properties.
- Coordination of the architectural, structural and geotechnical design teams from RIBA Stage 2 through to Stage 4, ensuring basement layout, pool hall configuration, plant room sizing, and escape routes complied with Building Regulations, local planning conditions and the client’s detailed brief for a high-end leisure facility.
- Preparation of a detailed employer’s requirements document, tender drawings and specifications for the basement box, pool shell, waterproofing systems (including combined Type A barrier and Type C drained cavity with sump pumps and battery backup), and M&E services associated with the pool, spa, and environmental control.
- Management of the competitive tender process to select a specialist basement contractor and a separate swimming pool specialist. This included issuing tender packages, responding to RFIs, organising site visits, analysing returned tenders, producing a tender report with recommendations, and supporting the client in final contractor appointments.
- Acting as contract administrator under a JCT Design & Build contract, including drafting contract particulars, issuing instructions, valuing variations, certifying interim payments, monitoring programme, and managing risk registers and early warning notices when unexpected ground conditions were encountered (including localised groundwater inflows and a previously unidentified manhole connection).
- Chairing monthly progress meetings and separate technical coordination meetings focused on structural sequencing, temporary works, waterproofing detailing, and pool integration. I maintained detailed action logs, updated programmes in collaboration with the main contractor, and ensured that the pool specialist’s requirements for shell tolerances, movement joints, and plant room space were captured early and respected during construction.
- Overseeing quality control inspections at key hold points: completion of underpinning and temporary works, waterproofing membrane installation, drainage channels and sump chambers, concrete pour for the pool shell, pressure testing of pool pipework, installation of vapour barriers and insulation, and commissioning of the environmental control and dehumidification system.
- Managing neighbour liaison and party wall surveyors’ information requests, including provision of method statements, vibration monitoring data, and settlement monitoring records to reassure adjoining owners and comply with party wall awards.
- Coordinating snagging, final inspections, and staged handover of the basement areas, including the pool hall and spa, ensuring that O&M manuals, as-built drawings, warranties (particularly for the waterproofing and pool shell), and maintenance schedules for the plant were complete and issued to both the client and their facilities management team.

Throughout the contract, my role was primarily managerial and advisory rather than hands-on construction. I did not design or install the filtration or chemical treatment systems, but I ensured that the appointed pool specialist carried appropriate professional indemnity cover and that their appointment terms were broadly back-to-back with the obligations placed on Hawthorne Residential Developments Ltd under the main contract and the client’s funding agreement.
